7, 2023, the UN said on Friday, following the death of another aid worker in an Israeli strike a day earlier.
UN spokesman Stephane Dujarric told reporters that a staff member from Medecins Sans Frontieres (MSF) was killed in a strike on Deir al-Balah, which also seriously injured four others while the team was waiting for a bus to go to an MSF hospital.
“This was the 14th staff member from Medecins Sans Frontieres to have been killed in Gaza since the start of this conflict on October 7,” Dujarric added.
MSF identified the victim as Omar Hayek, 42, an occupational therapist who had worked with the group since 2018.
“All staff were wearing MSF vests, clearly identifying them as medical humanitarian workers,” it said, expressing “deep sorrow and outrage” over his killing.
7), at least 562 aid workers have been killed, including some in the line of duty.
That includes 376 UN staff members,” he said.
The Israeli army has killed over 66,000 Palestinians, most of them women and children, in Gaza following a cross-border attack by Hamas on Israel on Oct.
The relentless bombardment has rendered the enclave all but uninhabitable and led to starvation and the spread of disease.
Last November, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant for war crimes and crimes against humanity in Gaza.

tavily.raw: {"query":"UNRWA staff casualties total number killed attacks since 2023 official statistics","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/anadoluagencyenglish/posts/more-than-300-staff-members-of-unrwa-have-been-killed-in-israeli-attacks-in-gaza/1023686586617024","title":"More than 300 staff members of UNRWA have been killed in Israeli ...","content":"More than 300 staff members of UNRWA have been killed in Israeli attacks in Gaza since October 2023, Since October 2023, 56,647 Palestinians","score":0.89720124,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.thenewhumanitarian.org/maps-and-graphics/2024/03/21/behind-numbers-gaza-unprecedented-aid-worker-death-toll","title":"The New Humanitarian | Behind the numbers: Gaza’s record aid worker death toll","content":"# Behind the numbers: Gaza’s unprecedented aid worker death toll. Israel’s siege of Gaza has killed more than 30,000 people, starved a population, and pushed hundreds of thousands to the brink of famine. They have been struck while at work in hospitals, in ambulances, or in aid convoys. As of 20 March, at least 196 humanitarians have been killed in the Occupied Palestinian Territories since October 2023, according to figures compiled by the Aid Worker Security Database (AWDS) – the aid community’s main source for tracking attacks on aid workers. One in every 100 UNRWA staff in Gaza has been killed – the highest staff death toll in UN history. Local staff have shouldered the brunt of the violence: All the aid workers killed in Gaza are national Palestinian staff, reflecting who’s responsible for the bulk of aid, and the sector’s continuing inability to extend the protection enjoyed by international staff to its frontline workforce.","score":0.87807417,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.un.org/unispal/document/unrwa-sitrep-184-15aug25","title":"UNRWA Situation Report #184 on the Humanitarian Crisis in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem - Question of Palestine","content":"# UNRWA Situation Report #184 on the Humanitarian Crisis in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem. *Days 669- 674 since the start of the war in the Gaza Strip.*. ### **The Gaza Strip**. **Gaza Humanitarian overview and response:**. Between 7 October 2023 and 13 August 2025, according to the MoH in Gaza, as stated by *OCHA*, at least **61,722** Palestinians have been reportedly killed in Gaza while **154,525** have been reported injured. As of 12 August 2025, **895\\*** incidents impacting UNRWA premises and the people inside them have been reported since the beginning of the war. UNRWA estimates that, in total, at least **845\\*** persons sheltering in UNRWA installations have been reported killed and at least **2,554\\*** injured since the start of the war. *\\*Since the start of the war in October 2023, the latest casualty figures are continuously under review as UNRWA gains access to locations that were previously inaccessible and as further verifications occur.","score":0.8512743,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attacks_on_humanitarian_workers","title":"Attacks on humanitarian workers - Wikipedia","content":"+ 2.1 War in Afghanistan (2001–2021)). + 3.1 Insurgency in the Maghreb (2002 – present)). + 17.2 War in Iraq (2013–2017)). # Attacks on humanitarian workers. **Attacks on humanitarian workers** are a leading cause of death among aid workers. Under international humanitarian law, deliberate violence is prohibited against protected persons, including humanitarian aid workers belonging to United Nations agencies, nongovernmental organisations (NGOs), and the International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement. The number of aid workers attacked has increased from 260 in 2008 to 595 in 2023. For the first 20 years of the AWSD, Afghanistan, South Sudan, Sudan, Somalia, and Syria were consistently the most dangerous places for aid workers to operate. In November 2024, the UN reported that 281 aid workers had been killed that year, making 2024 the deadliest year on record; 175 of the deaths occurred in Gaza. During the Global War on Terror, including the Iraq War and War in Afghanistan \"War in Afghanistan (2001–2021)\"), the number of attacks in the Middle East and Central Africa grew.","score":0.83549726,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.aidworkersecurity.org/incidents/report","title":"Major attacks on aid workers: Summary statistics","content":"Major attacks on aid workers: Summary statistics ; Total killed, 387, 297 ; Total injured, 308, 213 ; Total kidnapped, 138, 108 ; International victims, 23, 26","score":0.6989468,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.71,"request_id":"01b6f53d-d6b7-4d8e-83a7-3424b5f83557"}
